model_findings.description: chat_completions accepts arbitrary-length messages/content with no max_length, request-size middleware, or truncation; the resulting prompt is passed verbatim via stdin or embedded in argv (e.g. _gemini_backend line 280, _hermes_backend line 290, _claude_backend line 270) to long-running subprocesses, enabling trivial memory exhaustion or ARG_MAX failures from a single HTTP POST.

serve --openai accepts any --host (default 127.0.0.1) without enforcement, warning, or documentation that the proxy is unauthenticated; nothing prevents an operator from passing --host 0.0.0.0 and exposing the full agent surface to the network.

healthz invokes every _BACKEND_PROBE (each a 1-second subprocess --version) on every request with no caching, rate limit, or cheap static response, allowing an unauthenticated caller to trivially amplify CPU and process-table usage.

_codex_backend creates a delete=False NamedTemporaryFile before the try/finally that unlinks it; any exception between the with statement and the try (or an abrupt interpreter exit) leaves the file on disk.
